name: Bill Chase
abstract: Bill Chase (October 20, 1934 – August 9, 1974) was an American trumpeter and leader of the jazz-rock band Chase.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Chase, the Bill Evans Trio, and the Elvin Jones Quintet
Newport Jazz Festival: Chase, the Bill Evans Trio, and the Elvin Jones Quintet

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Moore
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Chase was on that band... I joined the band... I was there for three years
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Moore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Lennie taught Bill Chase and myself at Berklee in 1955... And so Chase and I learned to lock in on each other. As a lead player I’d be playing lead trombone, he’d be playing lead trumpet beautifully
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 27, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Espinosa
about_quotes.evidence: And in the section when I was there it was Bill Chase, myself, Gerry Lamy, Don Rader, and John Bennett and we were the trumpet section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Espinosa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 20, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: It had a nucleus, it was Bill Chase, Sal, myself and Nat and Chuck and that’s all we had.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: I kept Bill Chase with me... Chase was there every night, through the whole --

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Findley
about_quotes.evidence: And on Woody Herman’s band was Bobby Shew. And that’s when Bill Chase was playing. Bill was like the star of the Woody Herman band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Findley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 6, 2023
